ministry_department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
short_description: Promotes organic farming through cluster approach and provides financial assistance of ₹50,000 per hectare over 3 years.
detailed_description: PKVY is an elaborated component of Soil Health Management under Mission Organic Value Chain Development. The scheme promotes organic farming through adoption of organic villages by cluster approach and Participatory Guarantee System (PGS) certification. Financial assistance of ₹50,000 per hectare for 3 years is provided to farmers for organic inputs, certification, capacity building, and marketing support. The scheme aims to improve soil health, reduce chemical usage, and increase farmer income through premium prices for organic produce.
key_benefits: Financial assistance of ₹50,000 per hectare distributed over 3 years
Free organic farming training and capacity building programs
PGS certification support for organic produce
Marketing assistance and linkages for organic products
Cluster-based approach ensures better results and peer learning
Improved soil health and reduced input costs over time
other_conditions: Minimum cluster size of 50 acres (20 hectares) required
At least 50 farmers should form a cluster with contiguous land
Must commit to organic farming practices for minimum 3 years
Land should be suitable for organic farming
Cluster must adopt PGS certification process

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: 7/12 extract, land records, or lease agreement proving ownership/tenancy rights
Bank passbook or cancelled cheque for direct benefit transfer
List of all farmers in cluster with signatures and land details
Written consent to adopt organic farming for 3 years

application_steps_offline: Form a cluster of minimum 50 farmers with contiguous or nearby land parcels
Contact District Agriculture Officer or Krishi Vigyan Kendra for guidance
Prepare cluster proposal with complete farmer list and land details
Submit proposal to District Agriculture Office with all required documents
Attend mandatory orientation program on organic farming practices
Get PGS certification process initiated through local group
Receive financial assistance in three annual installments after verification

submission_locations.notes: Krishi Vigyan Kendras also provide assistance in cluster formation and proposal preparation

processing_time: 60 to 90 days for cluster approval and first installment

faq.question: Can individual farmers apply for PKVY?
faq.answer: No, farmers must form a cluster of minimum 50 farmers with at least 20 hectares of contiguous land to be eligible.

faq.question: What is PGS certification?
faq.answer: Participatory Guarantee System is a quality assurance system for organic products based on active participation of stakeholders and built on trust, social networks and knowledge exchange.

faq.question: How is the financial assistance disbursed?
faq.answer: The ₹50,000 per hectare is disbursed in three annual installments over 3 years directly to farmer's bank account.

common_rejection_reasons: Cluster size less than minimum 50 farmers or 20 hectares
Non-contiguous land parcels making cluster management difficult
Incomplete farmer list or missing documentation
Land not suitable for organic farming (highly degraded or contaminated)
Lack of commitment from farmers for 3-year organic farming
tips_for_successful_application: Form cluster with genuinely committed farmers interested in organic farming
Ensure land parcels are contiguous or in close proximity for better management
Get technical support from local agriculture department or KVK
Attend all training programs and follow organic farming guidelines strictly
Maintain proper records of organic inputs and farming practices
